title: chromolaena guiengolensis
intro: Chromolaena guiengolensis, also known as Eupatorium or False Boneset, is a plant species native to Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Chromolaena guiengolensis include relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce pain and inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used to make infusions, salves, and poultices for topical applications.
